commonIssues:
Problems with old DL numbers not appearing in the Sarathi database (Digital Backlog) or numbering format mismatches between older paper licenses and new smart cards.

How to fix: Visit the RTO where the license was originally issued and apply for a "Backlog Entry" to digitize your records. Carry your original paper license and Aadhaar for verification.

Applicants at Bengaluru RTOs (like Jayanagar or Electronic City) often fail the automated sensor-based tests on H-tracks and Parallel parking tracks.

How to fix: Enroll in a recognized driving school that has access to practice tracks. Familiarize yourself with the 10-minute time limit for the entire test circuit. Do not touch any sensor poles as it results in instant disqualification.

Applicants above 40 often have their renewal applications rejected because they upload a physical Form 1A instead of a digital one issued by a registered medical practitioner.

How to fix: Visit a government doctor or a registered private doctor who has an active login on the Parivahan portal. They must upload your medical clearance digitally for the Sarathi system to process your renewal.

Many citizens in Karnataka fall prey to fake websites that look like Parivahan but charge high "service fees" without providing any actual RTO service.

How to fix: Always ensure the URL ends in .gov.in. The only official portal is sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. Never pay through any private portal claiming to be the RTO.
